customer-followup: Sansan returns biz card data in batches — contact_id and card_id are different fields, use contact_id for CRM linkage
Chatwork rate limit is 300 requests per 5 minutes — batch follow-up messages to avoid hitting the cap
Backlog issue creation requires project_id and issueType_id which are numeric, not human-readable names
kintone-hubspot-contact-sync: kintone field codes are workspace-specific — hardcode the actual field codes, not display names
HubSpot contact properties are case-sensitive — 'Email' and 'email' are different fields
HubSpot rate limit is 100 requests per 10 seconds — batch kintone record reads to stay under the cap
kintone record numbers are auto-generated — never try to set them when creating records in HubSpot-to-kintone direction
zendesk-hubspot-support-to-crm: Zendesk uses cursor-based pagination (not offset) — follow the next_page URL, don't manually increment page numbers
Zendesk ticket comments include both public replies and internal notes — filter by 'public: true' before syncing to HubSpot
HubSpot association API is separate from contact/deal APIs — creating a ticket-contact link requires a second API call
Zendesk rate limit varies by plan (Team: 200/min, Pro: 400/min) — check your plan before setting batch sizes
salesforce-chatwork-deal-notify: Salesforce REST API requires your instance-specific URL (e.g., na1.salesforce.com), not a global endpoint
Chatwork message format is plain text only — no HTML, no markdown. Use [info] tags for structured layout
Salesforce OAuth refresh_token expires if unused for 90 days — set up a keep-alive if the workflow runs infrequently
Chatwork rate limit: 300 requests per 5 minutes. Batch deal notifications to avoid throttling
salesforce-slack-lead-routing: Salesforce SOQL queries have a 2000 record limit per query — use OFFSET or queryMore for larger lead pools
Slack user lookup by email may fail if the Salesforce lead email doesn't match the Slack workspace email
Salesforce lead assignment rules run server-side — if you reassign via API, assignment rules may override your change
Slack channel IDs are required (not channel names) — use conversations.list to resolve names to IDs first
marketo-salesforce-lead-sync: Marketo uses session-based auth — tokens expire in ~1 hour, always check token validity before batch operations
Marketo lead sync can be slow for large batches (>300 records) — use bulk extract API for initial sync, then incremental
Salesforce-Marketo field mapping is strict — field types must match exactly (Marketo string won't auto-convert to Salesforce number)
Marketo API rate limit is 100 calls per 20 seconds — the bulk API has separate limits
sendgrid-hubspot-email-track: SendGrid event webhook payloads are batched — a single POST may contain dozens of events, parse the array
SendGrid email addresses are case-insensitive but HubSpot treats them as case-sensitive in some contexts — normalize to lowercase
SendGrid events arrive with variable delay (seconds to minutes) — don't assume real-time delivery for HubSpot timeline updates
HubSpot engagement API for logging emails requires the contact vid — look up by email first
satori-kintone-lead-capture: SATORI MA webhook payloads use internal field IDs — map them to human-readable names before processing
kintone record creation requires all mandatory fields — SATORI may not provide all fields, set defaults for missing ones
Chatwork notification rate limit: 300 req/5min. If SATORI sends a burst of leads, queue the Chatwork messages
kintone query syntax is NOT SQL — use 'field_code = "value"' format, not WHERE clauses
b-dash-sendgrid-segment-email: b→dash segment export format may vary — verify the CSV/JSON structure before piping to SendGrid contact lists
SendGrid contact list updates are async — the API returns 202 Accepted, not immediate confirmation
Slack message limit is 4000 characters — truncate segment summary if the audience list is large
b→dash API documentation is limited — test endpoints in staging before production automation
zendesk-slack-escalation: Zendesk ticket priority field uses specific values: 'urgent', 'high', 'normal', 'low' — case-sensitive
Slack thread replies require thread_ts parameter — without it, escalation messages go to channel root, not the thread
Zendesk webhook triggers fire on every ticket update — add conditions to avoid duplicate Slack notifications on comment additions
intercom-kintone-support-log: Intercom API v2 merged the lead and user models — use 'contact' type for both, check 'role' field to distinguish
Intercom pagination uses cursor tokens — follow 'pages.next' URL, tokens expire if not used promptly
kintone bulk insert is limited to 100 records per request — batch Intercom conversation exports accordingly
Intercom conversation 'parts' include both messages and events — filter by 'part_type' to get only actual messages
channel-talk-hubspot-sync: Channel Talk webhook signatures must be verified — check the X-Signature header with your signing key
Channel Talk user profiles use 'profile.name' nested structure — flatten before mapping to HubSpot flat properties
HubSpot contact create vs update returns different status codes (200 vs 200 with vid) — handle both for idempotent sync
Channel Talk API rate limits are not well-documented — implement exponential backoff
make-salesforce-kintone-bidirectional: Make (Integromat) webhooks have a 30-second timeout — Salesforce bulk queries may exceed this, use scheduled polling instead
Bidirectional sync between Salesforce and kintone can cause infinite loops — add a 'last_synced_by' field to break cycles
Make scenario data passing between modules loses type information — numbers may become strings, add explicit type conversion
kintone field codes and Salesforce field API names use different conventions — maintain a mapping table in Make's data store
salesforce-sansan-contact-enrich: Sansan rate limit is strict: 100 requests per minute — batch Salesforce contact lookups before hitting Sansan
Sansan contact_id and card_id are separate — use contact_id for person-level data, card_id for specific business card images
Salesforce contact update requires the record ID — look up by email first, handle duplicates (Salesforce may have multiple contacts per email)
Sansan returns Japanese company names in full-width characters — normalize before matching with Salesforce Account names
salesforce-notion-deal-wiki: Notion API requires database_id for database operations — page_id is different. Use search API to find the right database
Salesforce Opportunity field values may contain special characters that break Notion rich text — sanitize before insertion
Notion property names must exactly match the database schema — 'Deal Name' != 'deal_name'. Check casing and spaces
Notion rate limit is 3 requests per second — queue Salesforce deal updates if batch processing
google-analytics-slack-traffic-alert: GA4 Data API uses 'properties/PROPERTY_ID' format — not the old UA-XXXXX tracking ID
GA4 API quota: 10 requests per minute per property for free tier — schedule alerts, don't poll frequently
Slack message formatting: use mrkdwn (not markdown) — *bold* works but **bold** doesn't
GA4 date ranges use YYYY-MM-DD format — relative dates like 'today' or 'yesterday' are also supported but may cause timezone issues
zendesk-kintone-ticket-mirror: Zendesk incremental export API is the efficient way to sync — don't use list-all-tickets for ongoing mirroring
kintone has a 100-record bulk insert limit — paginate Zendesk ticket exports into chunks of 100
Zendesk ticket custom fields use numeric IDs (not names) — pre-map IDs to kintone field codes
Zendesk timestamps are in UTC — convert to JST if kintone records need Japan timezone
intercom-slack-vip-alert: Intercom 'company' data is separate from 'contact' — you need the company association to determine VIP status by plan/revenue
Slack @mentions in messages require the user's Slack ID (not name) — look up via users.lookupByEmail first
Intercom webhook payloads for 'conversation.user.created' vs 'conversation.user.replied' have different structures
Intercom rate limit: 1000 requests per minute on most plans — generous but can be hit during bulk webhook replays
channel-talk-zendesk-escalation: Channel Talk conversation export includes all message types — filter for 'userMessage' type to exclude system messages
Zendesk ticket creation requires at least 'subject' and 'comment.body' — Channel Talk messages may lack a subject, generate one from first message
Channel Talk and Zendesk use different priority/severity scales — build an explicit mapping table
Zendesk returns the created ticket ID immediately — store it back in Channel Talk as a custom field for bidirectional reference
salesforce-backlog-customer-issue: Backlog project and issue type IDs are numeric — use the project/list and issue-type/list APIs to resolve human names to IDs
Salesforce Case record contains HTML in the description field — strip HTML tags before posting to Backlog (which uses markdown)
Backlog API requires either API key or OAuth — API key is simpler but has full access scope (no granular permissions)
Salesforce-to-Backlog field mapping: Salesforce 'Priority' values (High/Medium/Low) don't match Backlog's (2/3/4) — map explicitly
hubspot-calendly-gmail-sales: Calendly webhook payload includes invitee email — use this to look up the HubSpot contact, not the event name
Google Workspace (Gmail) API requires OAuth consent with mail.send scope — this is a sensitive scope requiring Google verification
Calendly event types have UUIDs — store the mapping between event type names and UUIDs in configuration
HubSpot meeting engagement logging requires the contact vid and timestamp — Calendly provides ISO 8601 timestamps, HubSpot expects Unix milliseconds
zendesk-slack-linear-support-to-bug: Linear API is GraphQL-only — use mutations for issue creation, not REST endpoints
Linear team IDs and label IDs are UUIDs — cannot use human-readable names directly in API calls
Zendesk ticket HTML content needs conversion to Linear markdown — strip HTML tags and convert basic formatting
Slack thread_ts is required for posting the Linear issue link as a reply — without it, the link goes to the channel root
shopify-klaviyo-segment-ecommerce: Shopify GraphQL Admin API uses cost-based rate limiting — a single complex query can consume your entire budget
Klaviyo API v3 uses cursor-based pagination — follow the 'next' link in response, don't manually paginate
Segment track calls require an anonymousId or userId — Shopify customer IDs need to be mapped consistently
Klaviyo list membership updates are async — the API returns 202, actual list population happens in the background
